title: Tapping to Trust Constant Replenishment
subtitle: A tapping practice for releasing the fear that what leaves will not return
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 61 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the clinging pattern: guarding every penny because money once spent might not come back. The reframe moves toward money as something that always returns and renews.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 61 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you find yourself hoarding money out of a vague fear of future scarcity rather than any specific bill or shortfall.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 61 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the clinging pattern: the sense that money once gone is gone for good, that a dry spell is always around the corner, that the responsible thing is to hold on tight rather than trust replenishment. The reframe moves toward money as something that always comes back.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I used to fear that money once gone is gone, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I thought income might dry up without warning, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I once clung to every penny out of fear, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I used to think there's a point where money stops.
Side of eye: I braced for dry spells.
Under eye: I tried to guard every penny.
Under nose: I worried about the next drought.
Chin: I stayed on alert for loss.
Collarbone: I'm ready to trust continuous flow.
Under arm: I release fear of running out.
Top of head: I welcome constant replenishment.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: Money always comes back and grows.
Side of eye: Every spend is matched with new supply.
Under eye: I trust life to refill every account.
Under nose: I relax into permanent abundance.
Chin: I love how quickly wealth renews.
Collarbone: I expect steady, never-ending inflow.
Under arm: I feel secure no matter what leaves.
Top of head: I live in a world of endless replenishment.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 61 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
